denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION
 Date of Notice: 05/06/2026
Cigna Open Access Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W100815657          Group #: GRP-1103
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-118132
Claim #: CLM-2026-193460
Date of Service (requested): 04/16/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Ridgeway Physician Services
 Dear Member,
 This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 04/18/2026 for Teprotumumab for thyroid eye disease related to Graves disease with ophthalmopathy (E05.00). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request. CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Teprotumumab for thyroid eye disease
Diagnosis (as submitted): Graves disease with ophthalmopathy (E05.00)
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria. Clinical policy applied: Coverage Policy 123 under Cigna Medical Coverage Policy (CPG/MM_ series) and Milliman Care Guidelines (MCG)
 EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in Coverage Policy 123 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Teprotumumab for thyroid eye disease is not considered evidence-based for Graves disease with ophthalmopathy (E05.00) under current medical policy. Published criteria do not support authorization. Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with not evidence based. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, including plain-language descriptions, as required by applicable federal standards. If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials. YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Many members use this information when preparing an appeal. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials. APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination through our internal appeals process. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W100815657) and reference UM-2026-118132. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination. Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above. Sincerely,
 Cigna Health Care Management
Medical Review Unit
clinical_context: The 49-year-old member has Graves disease with ophthalmopathy (E05.00). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Teprotumumab for thyroid eye disease recommended as the next appropriate step. CAS score 5/7 with diplopia; euthyroid on methimazole. Submitted documentation supports medical necessity for Teprotumumab for thyroid eye disease in the setting of Graves disease with ophthalmopathy (E05.00); the denial letter does not discuss those records or cite the objective findings in the file.
